summary:
The Consumer Protection Act, 2019 is a law that aims to protect the rights of consumers in India. It replaces the old Consumer Protection Act, 1986, and provides more power to customers by promoting transparency. The law requires e-commerce companies to display the country of origin of their products, and it also protects consumers from false advertisements. The government has introduced an advertising code to ensure that customers are not misled by false claims.

The law sets up independent bodies at the central and state levels to handle consumer complaints and take action against companies that violate consumer rights. These bodies have the power to investigate cases, recall goods, and impose penalties on companies that engage in unfair practices. Consumers can approach these bodies, known as commissions, at the district, state, and national levels to resolve their complaints. The ultimate goal of the law is to advise the government on consumer protection policies and ensure that consumers are treated fairly. Overall, the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 is designed to empower consumers and promote a fair and transparent marketplace in India.
